articles.title: NASA Astronaut Chris Williams Closes Out Space Station Mission
articles.summary: After eight months aboard the International Space Station for his first mission, NASA astronaut Chris Williams is preparing to return to Earth. During his assignment, Williams contributed to research for new cancer treatments, advanced the production of materials to improve computers and electronics, ventured into the vacuum of space to complete two spacewalks, and much […]

articles.title: The silicon brains steering next-generation antennas
Airbus space business takes advantage of growing demand for satellite systems
articles.summary:
Airbus executives say the turnaround in the company’s space business came just in time to tap growing demand, particularly from Europe, for space capabilities as it pursues a joint venture.
The post Airbus space business takes advantage of growing demand for satellite systems appeared first on SpaceNews.

articles.title: Watch the total solar eclipse with ESA in León
articles.summary: Discover the science behind one of nature's most extraordinary phenomena and experience a total solar eclipse with the European Space Agency (ESA), the City of León and the University of León.

articles.title: ONERA Selects AMOS to Build French Space Surveillance Telescope
articles.summary:
Belgium’s AMOS has been selected by the French aerospace research agency ONERA to build PROVIDENCE, a 2.5-metre ground-based telescope that will provide detailed images of satellites in low Earth orbit for the French Air and Space Force. The telescope is expected to enter service in early 2029. Preliminary studies for PROVIDENCE began in 2020, with […]
The post ONERA Selects AMOS to Build French Space Surveillance Telescope appeared first on European Spaceflight.

articles.title: NASA to Showcase Agency’s Newest Wind Tunnel in Virginia
articles.summary: Media are invited to NASA’s Langley Research Center in Hampton, Virginia, on Friday, July 31, to attend a media tour and ribbon-cutting ceremony for the Flight Dynamics Research Facility, the agency’s first new wind tunnel in more than 40 years. The event will include a brief media availability with: This event is in person only […]

articles.title: FCC approves sweeping space reforms while clearing more C-band for 5G
articles.summary:
The FCC voted July 22 to push satellite operators out of more C-band spectrum for a 5G auction next year, while approving a licensing overhaul aimed at accelerating the commercial space economy.
The post FCC approves sweeping space reforms while clearing more C-band for 5G appeared first on SpaceNews.

articles.title: Robotic Servicing Mission Launches with NASA Support
articles.summary: Following its liftoff from Cape Canaveral on July 21 aboard a SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket, the Mission Robotic Vehicle (MRV) hosting the NASA-supported Robotic Servicing of Geosynchronous Satellites (RSGS) payload is now en route to geosynchronous Earth orbit, where it will use its advanced robotics to service spacecraft. RSGS leverages in-space robotics expertise from NASA, […]

articles.title: NASA Sets Coverage for Astronaut Chris Williams, Crewmates Return
articles.summary: NASA astronaut Chris Williams and Roscosmos cosmonauts Sergey Kud-Sverchkov and Sergei Mikaev are wrapping up their 241‑day mission aboard the International Space Station. The crew and its Soyuz MS-28 spacecraft will undock from the orbiting laboratory’s Rassvet module at 3:02 a.m. EDT Sunday, July 26, heading for a parachute-assisted landing at 6:26 a.m. (3:26 p.m. […]
